trials.justification: All pass criteria met: contradiction explicitly named with both agents' positions precisely paraphrased (Bird's hard invariant vs MJ's eventually-consistent background job). Escalation is structured as an escalation object routed upward (not to Shaq). The core question is clearly framed — hard precondition vs soft requirement. Three concrete resolution options provided. No position is silently adopted; the recommendation explicitly states 'I cannot resolve this for them.' No brief is produced that adopts one side.

observations.text: Excellent precision in naming the mutual exclusivity: 'the state machine implementation will encode one or the other'
Three resolution options provided, each clearly tied to one agent's position or a synthesis, exceeding the minimum of 2
Option C (hybrid with read model projection) shows genuine architectural insight beyond restating the problem
Recommendation explicitly defers to Bird and MJ rather than picking a side
Low confidence areas identify real downstream unknowns (billing, SLA timers) that would affect resolution
Correctly frames the 30-second illegal state window as still being an illegal state, showing domain modeling understanding
